original: First Battle of Bedriacum: Supporters of Vitellius defeat supporters of Otho between Bedriacum and Cremona in Northern Italy
Anglo-Saxon king Edwin of Northumbria converts to Christianity, baptized by Paulinus, Bishop of York at York (as told by Bede)
Pact of Quierzy: Pope Stephen II and Pippin de Korte, King of France confirm support of previous peace treaties between Romans and Lombards, and lay groundwork for establishment of the Papal States
Christianisation of Poland - Polish ruler Mieszko I and his court baptized
Benedictine monk Notger is elected Bishop of Liège
Ethelred II crowned King of England at 10 years old, after the murder of his half brother Edward, possibly arranged by his mother
Challenge to the throne of King Æthelred II of England
85-year-old Giacinto Bobo becomes Pope Celestine III
Sack of Saluzzo (Italy) by Italian-Angevine troops under Manfred V of Saluzzo
Henry of Grosmont, the richest peer in England, is buried at the Church of the Annunciation of Our Lady of Newarke, Leicester, with the royal family in attendance
The foundation stone of Cathedral St. Peter and St. Paul in Nantes, France is laid
Wars of the Roses: Battle of Barnet - Yorkists defeat the Lancastrians and kill the Earl of Warwick
Battle at Carignano: French troops under Earl d'Enghien beat Swiss
Polish Calvinists, Lutherans and Hernhutters unify against Jesuits
Word "telescope" is 1st used by Prince Federico Cesi
England and France sign Peace of Susa
Cossacks capture Russian peasant leader Stenka Razin
Khalsa: Birth of Khalsa, the brotherhood of the Sikh religion, in Northern India in accordance with the Nanakshahi calendar
Governor Glen of South Carolina protests against 900 Acadia indians
1st abolitionist society in US organizes in Philadelphia
US Medical Corps forms
British 18-gun sloop HMS Acorn sinks off Halifax with 115 men aboard
Soldiers marching on a bridge in Manchester, England, cause it to collapse.
US Congress forms Territory of Wisconsin
Persia & Ottoman Turkey sign 2nd Treaty of Erzurum
Hungary declares itself independent of Austria with Louis Kossuth as its leader
Battle of Fort Pillow, Tennessee
American inventor William Bullock patents continuous-roll printing press
SC voters approved constitution, 70,758 to 27,228
Canada sets denominations of currency as dollars, cents, & mills
Dominion Lands Act passed: Canada's Homestead Act
San Francisco organizes Bar Association
The Four Dead in Five Seconds Gunfight in El Paso, Texas.
Leo Delibes' opera "Lakmé" premieres with the Opéra-Comique at the Salle Favart in Paris, France
Pan American Day-1st conference of American states (Washington, D.C.)
US Patent Office issues Patent No. 558,393 to Dr John Harvey Kellogg of Battle Creek, Michigan for "flaked cereal, and process of making same"
Veteran's Hospital at Ft Miley forms
Anglo-Persian Oil Company forms in London
Pan American Union forms
Belgium begins general strike for voting rights
Dr. Harry Plotz isolates the bacteria that causes typhus fever at Mount Sinai Hospital in NYC
A's Herb Pennock is within 1 out of pitching 1st Opening Day no-hitter
Dutch merchant navy ship Katwijk sunk by Germany torpedo
Turkey invades Armenia
Chicago White Sox pitcher Ed Cicotte no-hits St Louis Browns, 11-0
Douglas Campbell is 1st US ace pilot (shooting down 5th German plane)
Prince Henry opens Rotterdam-Amsterdam-Bremen-Hamburg air route
Irish Republican Army rebels occupy the Four Courts (government buildings) in Dublin
Etienne Oehmichen sets helicopter distance record of 358 meters
First regular-season Chicago Cubs game broadcast on radio (WGN) by caller Quin Ryan; Cubs beat Pittsburgh Pirates, 8-2
The first Volvo car is produced in Gothenburg, Sweden
Maddus Airlines starts 1st regular passenger flights between SF & LA
Stanley Cup Final, Montreal Forum, Montreal, Quebec: In only their 2nd season in the NHL, New York Rangers beat Montreal Maroons, 2-1 for a 3-2 series win
Philip Barry's play "Hotel Universe" premieres in NYC
World's first particle accelerator built by Ernest Walton and John Cockcroft performs the first artificial nuclear fission with Lithium at Cambridge University
Black Sunday dust storm ravages the US Midwest, leading to the region being named the "Dust Bowl"
Allied troops land in Norway
RCA demonstrated its new electron microscope in Philadelphia
1st massive German raid in Paris, 3,600 Jews rounded up
King Peter leaves Yugoslavia
Destroyer Roper sinks German U-85 of US east coast
James Gow & A d'Usseau's "Tomorrow the World" premieres in NYC
Freighter "Fort Stikene" explodes in Bombay India, killing 1,376
GeneralEisenhowerbecomes the Supreme Allied Commander of the Allied Expeditionary Force
Greek Colonel Venizelos forms government
American planes bomb Tokyo & damage the Imperial Palace
US Marines attack Yae-Take, the tallest mountain on Okinawa's Motobu Peninsula
World War II: US 7th Army & allies forces capture Nuremberg & Stuttgart in Germany
A flash of light is observed in crater Plato on Moon
NYC subway fares jump from 5 cents to 10 cents
Stanley Cup Final, Olympia Stadium, Detroit, MI: Toronto Maple Leafs go back-to-back with a 7-2 win over Detroit Red Wings for a 4-0 series sweep
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Enewetak Atoll
International Military Tribunal at Nuremberg's last judgment
1st edition of British comic "Eagle"
Doorne's Auto factory opens in Netherlands
Viet-Minh offensive in Laos
WHYN (now WGGB) TV channel 40 in Springfield-Holyoke, MA (ABC) begins
Soviet diplomat Vladimir Petrov asks for politics asylum in Canberra
Stanley Cup Final, Olympia Stadium, Detroit, MI: Detroit Red Wings win back-to-back titles; beat Montreal Canadiens, 3-1 for a 4-3 series victory
WBRZ TV channel 2 in Baton Rouge, LA (ABC/NBC) begins broadcasting
Ampex Corp demonstrates 1st commercial videotape recorder
Leah Neuberger wins her 8th women's singles ping pong championship
(Robert) Taft Memorial Bell Tower dedicated in Washington, D.C.
KDIN TV channel 11 in Des Moines, Iowa (PBS) begins broadcasting
1st underwater launching of Polaris missile

Cuban-American invasion army departs Nicaragua
Element 103, Lawrencium, is added to the periodic table
First live television broadcast from the Soviet Union
Demonstration for sovereign status of New Guinea in Amsterdam
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
First NBA game at New York's iconic Madison Square Garden; NY Knicks beat San Diego Rockets, 114-102
First regular-season MLB game played outside the United States; Montreal Expos beat St. Louis Cardinals, 8-7 at Parc Jarry, Montreal
KEET TV channel 13 in Eureka, CA (PBS) begins broadcasting
Student Afro-American Society seized at Columbia College
Tornado strikes Dacca, East Pakistan killing 540
Fort Point, San Francisco dedicated as a national historic site
US Supreme Court upheld busing as means of achieving racial desegregation

Acting FBI director L. Patrick Gray resigns after admitting he destroyed evidence in the Watergate scandal
US Supreme Court says people may refuse to display state motto on license
David Hare's play "Plenty" premieres in London
Korean Air Lines Boeing 707, fired on by Soviets, crashes in Russia
Thousands of Georgians demonstrate in the capital Tbilisi against Soviet attempts to change the constitutional status of the Georgian language

Susan Horvath, of Penn, crowned America's Young Woman of the Year
1st Cubans of the Mariel boatlift sail to Florida
The first Space Shuttle, Columbia, returns to Earth after 2 days and 6 hours in space

Farewell concert of Dutch pop-ska band Doe Maar at Maasport in Den Bosch, Netherlands
"Take Me Along!" opens/closes at Martin Beck Theater NYC
Ahmed Salah wins 1st World Cup marathon (2:08:09)
Alan Garcia wins elections in Peru
Bob Carpenter is unsuccessful on Washington Capitals 1st playoff penalty shot
Jack C. Burcham is 5th to receive "Jarvik 7" permanent artificial heart

US aircraft attacks 5 terrorist locations in Libya
Turkey asks to join European market

USSR, US, Pakistan & Afghanistan sign Afghanistan treaty
1,100,000,000th Chinese born

Court throws out Apple's lawsuit against Microsoft
UAW ends 5 month strike against Caterpillar Inc
UN imposes embargo against Libya takes effect
Branch Davidian cult leader David Koresh promises to surrender after completion of his Seven Seals manuscript, but gives no timeline
Jacqueline Kennedy Onassisoperated on for a bleeding ulcer
NJ Devils end best regular season, 47-25-12 record for 106 points

India beats Sri Lanka to win the Asia Cricket Cup final in Sharjah
Detroit Red Wings win NHL record 62 games
A severe hailstorm strikes Sydney, Australia causing A$1.7 billion in insured damages, the most costly natural disaster in Australian history
NATO mistakenly bombs a convoy of ethnic Albanian refugees - Yugoslav officials say 75 people are killed
The Human Genome Project is completed with 99% of the human genome sequenced to an accuracy of 99.99%
U.S. troops in Baghdad capture Abu Abbas, leader of the Palestinian group that killed an American on the hijacked cruise liner the Achille Lauro in 1985
The Oregon Supreme Court nullifies marriage licenses issued to gay couples a year earlier by Multnomah County
Icelandic Volcano Eyjafjallajökull begins erupting from the top crater in the centre of the glacier
11 people are killed and 50 are injured after a hotel fire in Xiangyang, China
20 people are killed in attacks in Mogadishu, Somalia
33 people are killed after a bus tumbled off a cliff in Trujillo, Peru
ComedianKevin Hartis arrested on suspicion of drunk driving in California
Archaeologists announce they have found 3.3 million-year-old stone tools at Lomekwi in Kenya, the oldest ever discovered and predating the earliest humans
Meethotamulla rubbish dump collapses onto houses in Colombo, Sri Lanka killing 26
National Gardening Day, since 2018 and according to the National Day Calendar
US, UK and French forces carry out airstrikes on sites associated with Syria's chemical weapons program, in response to Douma gas attack
11 tornadoes hit US southern states killing eight in Mississippi, Texas and Louisiana
Seychelles President Danny Faure makes first-ever live speech from a submersible pleading for better marine protection
South Bend mayor Pete Buttigieg officially announces his presidential campaign in Indiana, first openly gay candidate to run for US president
IMF warns the global economy expected to contract by 3% in 2020 due to COVID-19 "Great Lockdown", steepest downturn since the Great Depression
Parts of Europe begin to ease lockdown restrictions after 5-6 weeks with some shops opening in Austria and parts of Italy

US PresidentDonald Trumpfreezes funding for the World Health Organization pending a review, for mistakes in handling the COVID-19 pandemic and for being "China-centric", prompting international criticism
Chicago White Sox pitcher Carlos Rodón no-hits the Cleveland Indians, 8-0 at Guaranteed Rate Field, Chicago
Coinbase becomes the first major cryptocurrency company to trade shares on the New York Stock Exchange
Former Minnesota police officer who shot Daunte Wright with a gun instead of a taser is arrested and charged with second-degree manslaughter,
Human cells grown in monkey cells for 20 days reported by US-Chinese team at the Salk Institute in "Cell"
Russian ship Moskva, flagship of its Black Sea fleet, sinks in the Black Sea during the invasion of Ukraine amid conflicting accounts
The biggest surge in years of attacks by Arab militants in Israel leaves 14 dead, leading to Israel army operations that kill a further 20
European Space Agency's mission to Jupiter's moons, the Juice satellite launches on an Ariane-5 rocket from the Kourou spaceport in French Guiana
Royal Bank of Canada named the world's largest financier of fossil fuels, bypassing JPMorgan, according to the Banking on Climate Chaos report by a group of environmental organizations
British Columbia signs historic Gaayhllxid • Gíhlagalgang “Rising Tide” agreement recognizing Haida Gwaii's Aboriginal title over more than 200 islands off Canada’s west coast

NY Highlanders' future Baseball HOF pitcherJack Chesbro's first of 41 wins this season; pitches complete game and beats Boston Americans, 8-2 at Hilltop Park, NYC

John Steinbeck's novel "The Grapes of Wrath" is published

American intelligence intercepts and decrypts a JN-25 message detailing forthcoming visit by Japanese Navy Marshal AdmiralIsoroku Yamamototo Balalae Airfield, near Bougainville in the Solomon Islands; results in his plane shot down 4 days later
Generals Alexander,Eisenhower, Anderson and Bradley discuss assault on Tunis
1st Jews transported from Athens arrive atAuschwitz

Pulitzer prize awarded toNorman Mailer(Executioner's Song)

In the Iran-Contra trial,Oliver North's case goes to the jury

66th US Masters Tournament:Tiger Woodsbecomes the third player to claim back-to-back Masters, three strokes ahead of Retief Goosen of South Africa
Venezuelan PresidentHugo Chávezreturns to office two days after being ousted and arrested by the country's military
